subject: ap-world-history
title: Short Answer Question — AP World History

parts.prompt:
Identify one development that contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies in the
period before circa 1500.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for identifying ONE development before circa 1500 that contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies. CB-acceptable responses: (1) the Mongol conquests contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies across Eurasia; (2) warfare between states led to diffusion of gunpowder technologies; (3) some states employed foreign military specialists who could train their forces in new cannon and gunpowder use; (4) gunpowder technologies spread along trade routes such as the Silk Road and the Indian Ocean.

sample_responses.response: The Mongol conquests across Eurasia in the 13th century carried Chinese gunpowder technology westward — Mongol armies used early cannons in their sieges, and military specialists and smiths attached to Mongol forces transferred the technology to Korea, the Islamic world, and eventually Europe.
sample_responses.commentary: Identifies a specific pre-1500 development (Mongol conquests) and explains its role in diffusing gunpowder westward. CB-aligned.

sample_responses.response: Warfare between states — such as the Hundred Years' War in Europe or the Ottoman campaigns against Byzantine Constantinople culminating in 1453 — drove the adoption and refinement of gunpowder weapons, because each belligerent had strong incentives to copy or import effective technologies from rivals.
sample_responses.commentary: Identifies state-to-state warfare as a driver of gunpowder diffusion and illustrates with specific pre-1500 conflicts. CB-aligned.
States a fact without identifying any specific development that contributed to its spread.

required_elements: The Mongol conquests contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies.
alternatives.correct_example: The Mongol conquests contributed to the spread of gunpowder technologies.

required_elements: Warfare between states led to the spread of gunpowder technologies.
alternatives.correct_example: Warfare between states led to the spread of gunpowder technologies.

required_elements: Some states employed foreign military specialists who could train their forces in how to use new types of cannons and other gunpowder weapons.
alternatives.correct_example: Some states employed foreign military specialists who could train their forces in how to use new types of cannons and other gunpowder weapons.

required_elements: Gunpowder technologies spread along trade routes, such as the Silk Road and the Indian Ocean.
alternatives.correct_example: Gunpowder technologies spread along trade routes, such as the Silk Road and the Indian Ocean.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt:
Explain one way the use of military technologies affected the development of land-based
empires in the period circa 1450 to 1750.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for explaining ONE way the use of military technologies affected the development of land-based empires circa 1450–1750. CB-acceptable responses: (1) Muslim empires like the Ottomans and Mughals used gunpowder to expand territory by conquering weaker neighbors; (2) some European states built overseas empires using cannons against technologically less developed societies; (3) states used gunpowder weapons to reduce noble challenges to royal authority, often by building professional armies.

sample_responses.response: The Ottoman use of massive siege cannons — including the bombards that breached the walls of Constantinople in 1453 — allowed the empire to conquer and consolidate territory across Anatolia, the Balkans, and eventually Egypt, because no traditional fortification could resist them; gunpowder thus directly enabled Ottoman land-empire expansion.
sample_responses.commentary: Explains a CB-listed relationship (Muslim empires using gunpowder for expansion) with a specific, historically accurate example. CB-aligned.

sample_responses.response: European monarchs used gunpowder weapons, especially siege cannons against castle walls, to reduce the military independence of feudal nobles; this helped French and Spanish kings centralize authority and build larger standing armies loyal to the crown rather than to regional lords.
sample_responses.commentary: Explains the state-centralization effect of gunpowder weapons on land-based empires, matching a CB-listed acceptable response.
Does not explain any specific way military technology affected empire development.

required_elements: Muslim empires such as the Ottomans and Mughals used armed trade and gunpowder to expand their territories by conquering weaker neighboring states.
alternatives.correct_example: Muslim empires such as the Ottomans and Mughals used armed trade and gunpowder to expand their territories by conquering weaker neighboring states.

required_elements: Some European states were able to create overseas empires by using cannons and other gunpowder weapons against the technologically less developed societies they encountered.
alternatives.correct_example: Some European states were able to create overseas empires by using cannons and other gunpowder weapons against the technologically less developed societies they encountered.

required_elements: Many states were able to use gunpowder weapons to eliminate or reduce challenges to their authority from the nobility, for example by building or expanding their professional armies.
alternatives.correct_example: Many states were able to use gunpowder weapons to eliminate or reduce challenges to their authority from the nobility, for example by building or expanding their professional armies.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt:
Explain one way navigational technologies contributed to economic change in the period
circa 1450 to 1750.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.
scoring_points.official_rubric: Earns 1 point for explaining ONE way navigational technologies contributed to economic change circa 1450–1750. CB-acceptable responses: (1) improved compass designs enabled Europeans to sail to the Americas, eventually creating the Atlantic trade system; (2) knowledge of seasonal monsoon winds allowed European states to establish trading-post empires in the Indian Ocean; (3) improved astrolabes and better mapmaking techniques facilitated European exploration of the African coast, leading to coastal trading posts.

sample_responses.response: Improved compass designs and more reliable navigational instruments allowed European ships to cross the Atlantic repeatedly and predictably, which gave rise to the triangular trade linking Europe, West Africa, and the Americas — creating the economic system that moved sugar, enslaved people, and silver at unprecedented volumes.
sample_responses.commentary: Connects a specific navigational technology (compass) to a specific economic transformation (the Atlantic system). CB-aligned.

sample_responses.response: Europeans' adoption of lateen sails and their growing understanding of Indian Ocean monsoon wind patterns allowed Portuguese and Dutch ships to build trading-post empires — Goa, Malacca, the Banda Islands — that rerouted the lucrative spice trade away from Muslim intermediaries toward Atlantic markets.
sample_responses.commentary: Links specific navigational knowledge (monsoon patterns) to a specific economic outcome (European control of Indian Ocean trading nodes). CB-aligned.
Too vague — does not identify a specific navigational technology or explain the economic change it caused.

required_elements: Improved compass designs enabled Europeans to sail to the Americas, eventually creating the Atlantic system of trade.
alternatives.correct_example: Improved compass designs enabled Europeans to sail to the Americas, eventually creating the Atlantic system of trade.

required_elements: Knowledge of seasonal monsoon winds allowed European states to establish trading-post empires in the Indian Ocean.
alternatives.correct_example: Knowledge of seasonal monsoon winds allowed European states to establish trading-post empires in the Indian Ocean.

required_elements: Improved astrolabes and better mapmaking techniques facilitated European exploration of the African coast, which led them to set up trading posts on the continent.
alternatives.correct_example: Improved astrolabes and better mapmaking techniques facilitated European exploration of the African coast, which led them to set up trading posts on the continent.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.
